rationale: Both papers are directly about win-back customers and second-lifetime behavior: the citing study models repeat churn timing/reasons for reacquired customers, while the cited paper develops a joint reacquisition–second-tenure (SLTV) hazard framework. The citing note explicitly says it extends prior second-lifetime/churn research, and the cited work is a core prior model in that exact domain. Given this close conceptual and methodological overlap, this is not an obvious incidental mention.
